proposed_hypotheses.text: The continuous variable feature_080 (range 30-90, mean ~65, plausibly age in years) is positively associated with pfs_months across the cohort.
The 3-level ordinal variable feature_063 (values 0/1/2) is negatively associated with pfs_months — patients at higher feature_063 levels have shorter progression-free survival.

analyses.code: from scipy import stats; r, p = stats.pearsonr(df['feature_080'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=+0.7015 (p≈0). feature_080 is the strongest single continuous predictor of pfs_months.
Mean pfs_months by feature_063 level: 0=5.64, 1=4.44, 2=3.29. Pearson r=-0.320 (p≈0). Each level increase is associated with ~1.2 fewer months of PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Binary feature_056 (prevalence ~30%) is associated with shorter pfs_months (lower mean in feature_056=1 vs feature_056=0).
Binary feature_042 (prevalence ~35%) is associated with longer pfs_months (higher mean in feature_042=1 vs feature_042=0); feature_042 may represent a treatment.
Binary feature_048 (prevalence ~10%) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Binary feature_111 (prevalence ~70%) is associated with longer pfs_months.

analyses.code: from scipy.stats import ttest_ind; ttest_ind(df.loc[df.feature_056==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df.feature_056==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: feature_056=1 → 3.61, feature_056=0 → 5.15. Welch t-test p≈0; mean difference = -1.54 months.
Mean pfs_months: feature_042=1 → 5.41, feature_042=0 → 4.31. Welch t-test p≈0; mean difference = +1.10 months.
Mean pfs_months: feature_048=1 → 3.75, feature_048=0 → 4.80. p=1.5e-178; mean difference = -1.05 months.
Mean pfs_months: feature_111=1 → 4.86, feature_111=0 → 4.30. p=3.7e-143; mean difference = +0.57 months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 (binary, ~35%) and feature_040 (binary, ~18%) are negatively associated with pfs_months; feature_111 effect is positive.
feature_039 (binary, ~64%, possibly endocrine therapy) is positively associated with pfs_months in unadjusted analysis.

analyses.result_summary: feature_015=1 mean 4.32 vs 0 mean 4.88; mean diff -0.56 (p=6e-144). feature_040=1 mean 4.32 vs 0 mean 4.78; mean diff -0.46 (p=6e-70).
feature_039=1 mean 4.82 vs 0 mean 4.46; mean diff +0.36 (p=5.8e-59). Marginal effect significant but small.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Race (categorical feature_011 with 5 levels: white/hispanic/black/asian/other) shows mean differences in pfs_months across groups.
Insurance type (categorical feature_089 with 4 levels: medicare/private/medicaid/uninsured) shows mean differences in pfs_months across groups.

analyses.code: from scipy.stats import f_oneway; f_oneway(*[df.loc[df.feature_011==v,'pfs_months'] for v in df.feature_011.unique()])
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs by race: asian 4.74, black 4.72, hispanic 4.71, other 4.76, white 4.68 (range 0.08 mo). One-way ANOVA F=0.85, p=0.494. No significant marginal disparity in PFS by race.
Mean pfs by insurance: medicaid 4.71, medicare 4.69, private 4.68, uninsured 4.73 (range 0.05 mo). ANOVA F=0.31, p=0.818. No marginal disparity in PFS by insurance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable OLS regression for pfs_months including the top binary and continuous predictors, feature_080, feature_111, feature_042, and feature_019 retain positive coefficients while feature_063, feature_056, feature_048, feature_015, feature_040, feature_067, and feature_101 retain negative coefficients.
Even after adjustment for clinical and biomarker variables, race (feature_011) dummies remain significantly associated with pfs_months.

analyses.code: import statsmodels.api as sm; X = df[['feature_080','feature_063','feature_056','feature_042','feature_048','feature_015','feature_111','feature_040','feature_039','feature_034','feature_067','feature_019','feature_101']]; sm.OLS(df.pfs_months, sm.add_constant(X)).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted OLS R²=0.792. Coefficients (β, p): age β=+0.176/yr (p<1e-300); feature_063 β=-1.17/level; feature_056 β=-1.55; feature_042 β=+1.10; feature_048 β=-0.98; feature_015 β=-0.58; feature_111 β=+0.53; feature_040 β=-0.47; feature_067 β=-0.077/unit; feature_019 β=+0.476/unit; feature_101 β=-0.019/unit. feature_039 (β=+0.014, p=0.32) and feature_034 (β=+0.002, p=0.88) lose significance after adjustment, suggesting their marginal effects were confounded.

After adjustment, race dummies are not significant: black β=-0.028 (p=0.28), hispanic β=-0.012 (p=0.61), asian β=-0.012 (ref), other β=-0.012 (p=0.78), white β=-0.019 (p=0.38). Insurance dummies also non-significant (all p>0.5). Race and insurance do not predict PFS independent of clinical features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment-like variable feature_042 has a positive interaction with feature_111: the PFS benefit of feature_042=1 vs feature_042=0 is much larger when feature_111=1 than when feature_111=0.
feature_042 has a negative interaction with feature_015: the PFS benefit of feature_042 is attenuated or absent when feature_015=1.
feature_042 has a negative interaction with feature_040: the PFS benefit of feature_042 is attenuated when feature_040=1.

analyses.result_summary: Stratified means: feature_111=0/feature_042=0 → 4.30, /feature_042=1 → 4.29 (no benefit). feature_111=1/feature_042=0 → 4.31, /feature_042=1 → 5.89 (+1.58). Adjusted interaction coefficient β(t × hr)=+1.56, p≈0. Treatment effect concentrates entirely in feature_111=1.

Stratified means: feature_015=0/feature_042=0 → 4.31, /feature_042=1 → 5.97 (+1.66). feature_015=1/feature_042=0 → 4.32, /feature_042=1 → 4.35 (+0.03). Adjusted interaction β(t × vis)=-1.67, p≈0. feature_015=1 abolishes treatment benefit.

Stratified means: feature_040=0/feature_042=0 → 4.31, /feature_042=1 → 5.66 (+1.35). feature_040=1/feature_042=0 → 4.32, /feature_042=1 → 4.31 (-0.01). Adjusted interaction β(t × her)=-1.34, p≈0. feature_040=1 also abolishes treatment benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a 3-way interaction model, the PFS benefit of feature_042 is concentrated in patients with feature_111=1 AND feature_040=0; the 3-way interaction term feature_042 × feature_111 × feature_040 is negative and large.

analyses.result_summary: 3-way interaction t × hr × her: β=-1.91, p≈0. Pairwise t × hr β=+1.91 (p≈0); 3-way reverses it for feature_040=1 patients. Cell means feature_111=1/feature_040=0/feature_042=1 → 6.24 (vs 4.31 untreated). All other cells flat ~4.3.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Similarly, feature_042 × feature_111 × feature_015 3-way interaction is large and negative: PFS benefit of feature_042 is concentrated in feature_111=1 AND feature_015=0 patients.

analyses.code: Set up t_hr, t_vis, hr_vis, t_hr_vis interaction terms in OLS with covariates
analyses.result_summary: 3-way β(t × hr × vis) = -2.40, p≈0. Cell means feature_111=1/feature_015=0/feature_042=1 → 6.68 vs untreated 4.30 (+2.38). feature_111=1/feature_015=1/feature_042=1 → 4.36 vs 4.33 (no benefit). feature_015 abrogates treatment benefit specifically in HR+ patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The continuous variable feature_101 (range 1-100, mean 15.5) negatively interacts with feature_042: each unit increase in feature_101 reduces the PFS benefit of feature_042.
feature_101 also negatively interacts with feature_111: feature_111=1 advantage shrinks as feature_101 increases.

analyses.result_summary: β(feature_042 × feature_101) = -0.053, p≈0 (highly significant). For each unit increase in feature_101, feature_042 benefit decreases by 0.053 months.
β(feature_111 × feature_101) = -0.026, p=1.1e-165. feature_111 advantage attenuates as feature_101 increases.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The 3-way interaction feature_042 × feature_111 × feature_101 is negative: among feature_111=1 patients, the PFS benefit of feature_042 is largest when feature_101 is low and disappears as feature_101 increases.

analyses.result_summary: β(t × hr × feature_101) = -0.075 per unit feature_101 (p≈0). At feature_111=1 with feature_101=0, treatment β=+2.73; benefit declines by ~0.075 months per unit of feature_101 and is essentially zero by feature_101≈36.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within feature_111=1 patients, the absolute PFS gain from feature_042 is largest in the lowest feature_101 quartile and approaches zero in the highest quartile; within feature_111=0, no benefit at any feature_101 level.

analyses.result_summary: Within feature_111=1: feature_042 effect by quartile of feature_101 (Q1→Q4): +2.62, +2.68, +0.96, +0.01 months. Within feature_111=0: -0.04, +0.07, -0.02, -0.07 (all near zero). Confirms strong feature_111 × feature_101 modification of feature_042 effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Race (feature_011) is associated with receipt of treatment feature_042 — i.e., the proportion of patients with feature_042=1 differs across race categories.
Insurance (feature_089) is associated with receipt of treatment feature_042.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_011')['feature_042'].mean(); chi-square / multinomial logistic adjusted for clin
analyses.result_summary: Marginal feature_042 prevalence by race: asian 0.345, black 0.351, hispanic 0.351, other 0.337, white 0.348 (range 0.014). In feature_111=1 subgroup adjusted for age/stage/feature_040/feature_015, all race dummies p>0.5. No measurable disparity in treatment receipt by race.

feature_042 prevalence by insurance: medicaid 0.349, medicare 0.350, private 0.348, uninsured 0.346 (range 0.004). Adjusted logistic regression in feature_111=1: all insurance dummies p>0.5. No disparity in treatment receipt by insurance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the responsive subgroup (feature_111=1, feature_015=0, feature_040=0), the treatment effect of feature_042 differs by race (i.e., race × feature_042 interaction is significant).
Among the responsive subgroup, the treatment effect of feature_042 differs by insurance type.

analyses.result_summary: Crude treatment benefit by race in responsive subgroup: asian +2.98, black +2.89, hispanic +2.96, other +2.87, white +2.89 months — all very similar. Adjusted race × feature_042 interactions all p>0.09 (white p=0.090, others p>0.37). No meaningful effect modification by race.

analyses.code: sub = responsive subgroup; insurance × treatment interaction OLS
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted insurance × feature_042 interactions all p>0.15 (medicare p=0.224, private p=0.153, uninsured p=0.552). No effect modification by insurance type.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_039 is highly correlated with feature_111 such that, after adjustment, feature_039 has no independent effect on pfs_months and adds nothing to feature_111 in the model.
feature_034 is mutually exclusive with feature_040 (feature_034=1 implies feature_040=0); within feature_040=0 patients, feature_034 has no independent effect on pfs_months after adjustment.

analyses.result_summary: feature_039 prevalence: 0.151 in feature_111=0 vs 0.851 in feature_111=1; Pearson r(feature_111,feature_039)=0.669. Adjusted β(feature_039)=+0.014 (p=0.32). feature_039 × feature_111 interaction β=+0.024 (p=0.45). No independent or modifying effect.

feature_034=1 occurs only when feature_040=0 (perfect mutual exclusion). Within feature_040=0 (n=40,940): mean pfs feature_034=1 vs 0 is 4.77 vs 4.79; adjusted β(feature_034)=+0.001 (p=0.92). No effect on PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effect of feature_042 is modified by patient age (feature_080): treatment benefit decreases with increasing age.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted β(feature_080 × feature_042) = -0.0016 per year, p=0.128. Treatment effect is statistically indistinguishable across the age range; no meaningful age modification of treatment benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The absolute treatment effect of feature_042 differs across feature_063 levels (stage-like ordinal): treatment provides larger absolute benefit at higher feature_063 levels (interaction is negative since feature_063 lowers PFS) OR a different absolute benefit at each level.

analyses.result_summary: β(feature_063 × feature_042) = -0.018 per stage level, p=0.263. Stratified means show similar absolute benefit ~+1.1 mo at every feature_063 level (0: 5.25→6.37; 1: 4.06→5.16; 2: 2.88→4.03). No interaction — treatment effect is approximately additive across stage.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The relationship between feature_080 (age) and pfs_months is nonlinear: a quadratic age term is statistically significant after adjustment for clinical covariates.

analyses.code: df['age2']=df.feature_080**2; OLS with linear+quadratic age and key covariates
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted β(feature_080) = +0.097, β(feature_080²) = +0.0006 per year². Quadratic term p=1.3e-59 (highly significant). Age effect on PFS is convex — accelerates at older ages — though linear effect dominates.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous laboratory features (feature_065 with range 2.5-6.3, feature_006 7.3-11.8, feature_007 128-152, feature_035 6-18, feature_046, feature_059, feature_087, feature_088, feature_002, feature_108) are significantly associated with pfs_months after adjustment for clinical features.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS adding 10 lab-like features to the core multivariable model
analyses.result_summary: After adjustment, none of these labs reach p<0.05: feature_065 β=+0.015 (p=0.15), feature_006 β=-0.011 (p=0.26), feature_007 β=-0.001 (p=0.68), feature_035 β=+0.0003 (p=0.91), feature_046 β=+0.002 (p=0.23), feature_059 β=+0.0002 (p=0.16), feature_087 β=-0.004 (p=0.12), feature_088 β=+5e-5 (p=0.93), feature_002 β=+0.0002 (p=0.67), feature_108 β=-0.0002 (p=0.49). All near-zero — no incremental prognostic value.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of continuous variable feature_067 (range 0-24.6, mean 3.84) on pfs_months is modified by feature_111 — specifically, the negative effect of feature_067 is larger in feature_111=1 patients.

analyses.result_summary: β(feature_067) = -0.077 (main, p<1e-300). β(feature_067 × feature_111) = +0.011 (p=0.06, NS). No reliable modification: feature_067 is uniformly negatively associated with PFS regardless of feature_111 status.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_019 (continuous, range 1.5-5.5, mean 3.8) is independently and positively associated with pfs_months and its effect is modified by feature_048 (interaction is negative).

analyses.result_summary: Main effect β(feature_019)=+0.476 per unit (p≈0). β(feature_019 × feature_048) = -0.099 per unit, p=0.0052 (significant). Among feature_048=1 patients the positive effect of feature_019 is attenuated by ~0.10 per unit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Race-stratified mean PFS in the maximally responsive subgroup (feature_111=1, feature_015=0, feature_040=0) is comparable across race categories, with similar treatment benefit ~3 months in each.

analyses.result_summary: Within responsive subgroup (n=18,681), treatment benefit (mean pfs_months treated minus untreated): asian +2.98 (n=383 vs 724); black +2.89; hispanic +2.96; other +2.87; white +2.89. Differences across races trivial (≤0.11 months).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The overall variance of pfs_months differs across race categories.

analyses.code: from scipy.stats import levene; levene(*[df.loc[df.feature_011==v,'pfs_months'] for v in df.feature_011.unique()])
analyses.result_summary: Standard deviations of pfs_months by race: white 2.498, asian 2.496, hispanic 2.477, black 2.511, other 2.482. All within 0.04. Differences trivial. (Levene-style heterogeneity not significant given the means/SDs).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with feature_111=0 (likely the receptor-negative subgroup), no biomarker-level interactions with feature_042 produce a clinically meaningful PFS gain — treatment is uniformly ineffective in this subgroup.

analyses.code: sub0 = df[df.feature_111==0]; t-test feature_042 effect within all biomarker strata
analyses.result_summary: In feature_111=0 (n=15,114), mean pfs feature_042=1 (n=5,234) vs feature_042=0 (n=9,880) is 4.29 vs 4.30 (diff -0.01 mo, p=0.86). Within all feature_101 quartiles, all feature_015 levels, and all feature_040 levels, no PFS benefit from feature_042 in feature_111=0 patients. Treatment is biomarker-restricted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable OLS for pfs_months that includes the 13 main predictors plus 4 interaction terms (feature_042×feature_111, feature_042×feature_111×feature_101, feature_042×feature_111×feature_015, feature_042×feature_111×feature_040) explains ≥85% of the variance in pfs_months.

analyses.code: OLS with main effects + 4 interaction terms above
analyses.result_summary: Full interaction model R² = 0.8894 (vs 0.792 without interactions). Interaction coefficients: t × hr = +3.90 (p≈0), t × hr × feature_101 = -0.075 (p≈0), t × hr × feature_015 = -2.39 (p≈0), t × hr × feature_040 = -1.90 (p≈0). Adding interactions captures ~10 additional percentage points of variance — treatment benefit is highly heterogeneous and predictable from the biomarker constellation.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across all of the previous analyses, sociodemographic variables (race feature_011 and insurance feature_089) show no marginal effect on pfs_months, no adjusted effect on pfs_months, no effect on receipt of feature_042 treatment, and no effect modification of feature_042 — i.e., this dataset shows no detectable racial or insurance-based disparities in either treatment access or outcomes.

analyses.code: Compilation of all race/insurance analyses across iterations 4, 5, 12, 13, 21, 22
analyses.result_summary: Race: marginal ANOVA on pfs p=0.49; adjusted dummies all p>0.28; treatment receipt by race ANOVA p=0.94; race × feature_042 interaction in responsive subgroup p>0.09 for all dummies; pfs SD by race differs by ≤0.04. Insurance: marginal ANOVA p=0.82; adjusted p>0.59; treatment receipt by insurance not significant; insurance × feature_042 in responsive p>0.15. Overall, sociodemographic factors are essentially orthogonal to clinical outcomes and treatment receipt in this de-identified cohort.
